title: crataegus monogyna var. quercifolia
intro: Crataegus monogyna var. quercifolia, also known as the Oak-leafed Hawthorn, is a variety of the Crataegus monogyna plant, which is a species of hawthorn native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and cardiotonic properties, which can help to lower blood pressure, reduce the risk of heart disease, and improve cardiovascular health. The health benefits of Crataegus monogyna var. quercifolia include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and acting as an antispasmodic to relieve muscle cramps and spasms. Herbal preparations of this plant include tea, capsules, and tinctures, which can be used to support heart health and overall well-being.
